scenes.subtitle: The Fortress

scenes.title: The Generator
scenes.subtitle: Mandrake's Deal
scenes.readAloud:
The generator room is deep in the fortress basement — a repurposed Clone Wars power unit, cables snaking out through the walls like arteries. Through the grated floor, you can feel the hum in your teeth. This one machine powers everything: the turrets on the walls, the laser fences penning Mandrake's people, the security doors sealing the detention block, the sensor grid watching every corridor.

Kill this, and the whole fortress goes blind.
scenes.gmNotes:
This is the tactical linchpin of the entire infiltration. The generator encounter has four-purpose significance — the GM should make the payoff chain EXPLICIT. When sabotaged, it simultaneously:

(1) Kills the perimeter turret defenses — clearing Maya's extraction flight path
(2) Drops the laser fences penning indigenous prisoners — enabling Mandrake's rescue
(3) Unlocks the security doors blocking the detention area — opening the path to Varth
(4) Blinds the sensor grid — creating chaos cover for everyone

If the heroes honor Mandrake's deal, this is where they pay it off. If they skip it, turrets stay active (Maya's extraction becomes near-suicidal), prisoners stay penned (Mandrake is betrayed), and security remains fully operational.

The generator room has 2 mercenary guards (bored, playing cards) and a Clone Wars-era security droid that activates if the generator is tampered with.

After the generator blows, the fortress alarm sounds. This is the point of no return — from here, it's a clock. The heroes need to reach the detention block before the garrison reorganizes.

npcs.name: Generator Guards
npcs.notes: Bored guards playing cards in the generator room. Not expecting trouble.
npcs.behavior: Startled and slow to react. They assumed no one could get this deep without triggering an alarm.
dialogue: "What the— how did you get in here?"
"Nobody told us about any inspection—"

scenes.hazards: Clone Wars-era generator — volatile if handled wrong. Security droid activates on tampering. Explosion risk on failure.

decisions.choice: Sabotage the generator per Mandrake's deal
decisions.consequence: Four-purpose payoff: kills turrets (Maya can extract), drops laser fences (prisoners freed), unlocks security doors (detention block opens), blinds sensor grid (chaos cover). Alerts the garrison but cripples their infrastructure.

decisions.choice: Skip the sabotage and head straight for Varth
decisions.consequence: Faster but turrets remain active (Maya's extraction becomes extremely dangerous), prisoners remain penned, Mandrake is betrayed. Full security remains active.

decisions.choice: Attempt a partial sabotage — selective systems only
decisions.consequence: Requires Legendary tech result. If achieved, heroes can choose which systems fail in what order. If not, full sabotage or nothing.

encounters.name: Generator Room Approach
encounters.trigger: Heroes reach the generator sublevel
encounters.description: Two mercenary guards in the generator room, playing cards. A Clone Wars security droid sits dormant in a charging cradle near the generator housing. The room hums with power.
encounters.tactics: Stealth takedown of guards avoids triggering the droid. Loud approach activates it. The droid is ancient but functional.

encounters.name: Generator Sabotage
encounters.trigger: Heroes attempt to sabotage the generator
encounters.description: The fortress generator is a repurposed Clone Wars power unit. Sabotaging it produces a four-purpose payoff chain. The GM should narrate each system going down in sequence for maximum dramatic impact: lights flicker, turrets whine down, laser fences spark and die, security doors clunk open, sensor screens go dark.
encounters.tactics: Tech check for clean sabotage. Failure = immediate detonation (still works but heroes caught in blast). Success = controlled shutdown with time to clear. The four-purpose chain should be narrated explicitly.

environmentMechanics.name: Clone Wars Security Droid
environmentMechanics.trigger: Activated by tampering with the generator or by combat noise
environmentMechanics.effect: An ancient but functional battle droid guards the generator. Slow but heavily armored for its era.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Stealth takedown of guards avoids activating it. Tech check can reprogram it. Destroying it is noisy.

environmentMechanics.name: Generator Volatility
environmentMechanics.effect: Uncontrolled detonation. Same fortress-wide effects but heroes caught in blast radius.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Field Toolkit enables clean sabotage. Without one, the check is harder.

intel: Generator sabotage produces four-purpose payoff: turrets, fences, doors, sensors
The Clone Wars tech powering this fortress predates the Empire by years — Skarn repurposed an old Separatist outpost
connections: Vazus Mandrake (deal honored — or betrayed — here)

pacing.openingBeat: The generator room. A repurposed Clone Wars power unit humming in the fortress basement.
pacing.risingAction: Guards to deal with. The security droid. Getting to the generator housing.
pacing.climax: The sabotage itself: the four-purpose payoff chain. Turrets die, fences drop, doors unlock, sensors blind. Mandrake moves to free his people.
pacing.resolution: The alarm sounds. The fortress goes dark. The clock is running — reach Varth before they reorganize.

disciplineChallenges.context: Neutralizing the generator room guards without triggering the security droid.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll on approach. Failure activates the droid and alerts nearby patrols.

control.failure: Guards shout an alarm. The security droid activates. Nearby patrols start converging.

effect.fleeting: Guards neutralized but one gets a partial shout. The droid's charging cradle flickers — it may activate soon.
effect.masterful: Clean takedown. Guards down silently. Droid remains dormant.
effect.legendary: Guards down without a sound. The PC also notices the droid's power cable — yanking it prevents activation entirely.

disciplineChallenges.context: Sabotaging the fortress generator. Kills turrets, drops fences, unlocks doors, blinds sensors. Four-purpose payoff — the tactical linchpin of the entire infiltration.
disciplineChallenges.isGated: Requires Field Toolkit for clean sabotage
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Always roll if attempted. Major tactical decision. Narrate each system going down.

control.failure: Immediate detonation. Heroes caught in blast radius. Same fortress-wide effects but at personal cost.
control.mastery: The sabotage is so clean it looks like equipment failure, not sabotage. No one suspects intruders — they think it's a mechanical fault.

effect.fleeting: Delayed detonation — heroes get 1 round to clear the area. Fortress systems go down.
effect.masterful: Clean sabotage with controlled shutdown — heroes get clear, fortress goes dark systematically.
effect.legendary: Precision sabotage: the generator fails in sequence, taking down systems one at a time. Heroes can choose WHICH systems fail first (turrets first? fences first? comms first?).

disciplineChallenges.context: Getting clear of the generator room when it blows. Debris, shrapnel, collapsing ceiling sections.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll immediately after sabotage. Only on fleeting tech result or failure.

control.failure: Caught in the blast. Significant damage. Knocked prone. Ears ringing.

effect.fleeting: Dive behind cover just in time. Minor bruising, ears ringing, but functional.
effect.masterful: Clean exit from the blast radius. No injuries.
effect.legendary: The PC grabs a slower ally and shields them both behind a blast door. No one hurt.

scenes.title: The Detention Block
scenes.readAloud:
You blow the door to the holding cell expecting a broken man. Instead, you find Admiral Gilder Varth sitting comfortably, gambling with a confused guard and packing a bag.

He looks up with a charming, tired smile and flips a credit chip in greeting. "You're late. I had credits on the second moonrise."
scenes.gmNotes:
Varth is arrogant, charming, and unbothered. He treats the rescue like a hired service. He is ready to leave immediately — no physical items to retrieve. His authentication key for the encrypted account is memorized, keeping him in permanent control of his leverage.

Key reveal: he didn't defect for moral reasons — he embezzled a fortune and needs the heroes to get him out. He uses nicknames: 'Kid,' 'Pilot,' 'Heavy.' This is the heroes' first real interaction with Varth — establish his 'Cool Uncle' persona.

HIS PITCH: Once freed, Varth explains: the money is locked inside Varga's financial network. He has the authentication key memorized, but Varga moved the account after Varth was captured. They need to get inside Varga's operation to find the routing data. This sets up Adventure 2.

IMPORTANT: Varth carries nothing physical. His authentication key is memorized — not on any device. His value is entirely in what he knows. This is a deliberate narrative choice — it makes him indispensable and impossible to cut out of the deal.

npcs.name: Admiral Gilder Varth
npcs.notes: The campaign's central NPC. Charming, cynical, self-serving. His authentication key for the encrypted Varga account is memorized — not stored on any device.
npcs.behavior: Treats the rescue like a hired service. Completely unbothered. Gambling with his guard when the heroes arrive. Uses nicknames: 'Kid,' 'Pilot,' 'Heavy.' Establishes his 'Cool Uncle' persona immediately. No baggage to grab — he's ready to walk.
dialogue: "You're late. I had credits on the second moonrise."
"I appreciate the dramatic entrance. Shall we go? I've been looking forward to fresh air."

"The money? It's locked inside Varga's network. I have the authentication key" — he taps his temple — "right here. The Hutt moved the account after I was captured. We need to get inside his operation to find the routing data."
"Call me Varth. Everyone does. Even people who want me dead — which is most of them."
npcs.intel: Varth embezzled a fortune from Project Leviathan through Varga the Hutt's supply chain. His authentication key for the encrypted account is memorized — not stored on any physical device. This keeps him in control of his leverage and prevents anyone from stumbling onto it. He didn't defect for moral reasons. He's a grifter in an admiral's uniform.

encounters.tactics: Varth controls the social terrain. He's the one with all the answers and he knows it. He's charming but transactional — everything is a deal.

environmentMechanics.name: Draco Clock (Continued)
environmentMechanics.effect: The clock keeps ticking. Every minute spent with Varth is a minute closer to Draco's arrival.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Move fast. Grab Varth and go.

environmentMechanics.effect: Heavier guard presence than other fortress sections. Automated cell locks. Surveillance cameras.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Generator sabotage (if completed in Scene 4) disables most of this — security doors unlocked, sensors blind.

disciplineChallenges.context: Reading Varth's true motives when you meet the man you came to rescue. Is the charm genuine?
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll if any PC is suspicious. Critical long-term consequence.

control.failure: Varth seems genuine — roguish, grateful, trustworthy. The charm lands.
control.mastery: The PC catches a micro-expression when Varth mentions Denia — a flash of something calculating, possessive. He doesn't see Denia as a person.

effect.fleeting: Something is off but can't pinpoint it. Varth is a little TOO smooth for a man who's been in a cell.
effect.masterful: Varth is genuinely self-serving. His charm is practiced, not natural. He's reading the PCs as much as they're reading him.
effect.legendary: Varth's gratitude is real but conditional — he's already calculating how to use the PCs. His "rescue" pitch was rehearsed. He's been waiting for this.

disciplineChallenges.context: Negotiating better terms for the rescue — a bigger cut of whatever Varth is offering.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll if PCs push for better deal.

control.failure: Varth's terms stand. "You'll get what I promised. That's how this works."

effect.fleeting: Minor concession — Varth promises priority access to intel once they reach his contacts.
effect.masterful: Varth agrees to a larger split. "You've earned it. Besides, there's plenty where this leads."
effect.legendary: Varth offers full partnership. "I need capable people. Not hired guns — partners. The thing I'm pointing you at is bigger than any one score." This is the hook into the campaign arc.

disciplineChallenges.context: Examining Varth's cell environment. For a prisoner, things look suspiciously comfortable. What has Varth been doing in here?
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll if PCs look around the cell instead of rushing out. Seeds long-term suspicion.

control.failure: It's a cell. Nothing unusual jumps out.

effect.fleeting: The cell is oddly well-maintained. Varth has clearly had privileges — extra blankets, better food rations.
effect.masterful: Tally marks on the wall don't match Varth's claimed time in custody. He's been here longer than he let on — or he inherited someone else's marks.
effect.legendary: Hidden under the bunk: a crude cipher written on cloth. Varth has been communicating with someone OUTSIDE the fortress. He didn't just wait to be rescued — he's been working.

disciplineChallenges.context: Building rapport with Varth during the extraction. He's charming and transactional — can the heroes establish genuine connection or is this purely business?
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll during the walk to extraction. Varth tests the heroes with banter and probing questions.

control.failure: Varth stays transactional. He's polite but clearly views the heroes as hired help. Uses professional distance.

effect.fleeting: Varth warms slightly. He drops a nickname — 'Kid,' 'Pilot,' or 'Heavy' — showing he's been watching and categorizing them.
effect.masterful: Genuine warmth. Varth tells a personal anecdote about his time in the cell. The 'Cool Uncle' persona starts to solidify. He remembers their names.
effect.legendary: Varth drops his guard for a moment. A flash of genuine gratitude — not performed, real. Then it's gone, covered by a quip. But the PC saw it. There's a person under the performance.

disciplineChallenges.context: If the heroes try to conceal their own motives or background from Varth. He reads people for a living — lying to him is hard.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll if any PC tries to mislead Varth about who they are or why they're here.

control.failure: Varth sees through it immediately. He doesn't call it out — he just files it away. The PC won't know they've been made.

effect.fleeting: Varth accepts the surface story. He doesn't seem to dig deeper — for now.
effect.masterful: Varth buys the cover completely. He adjusts his pitch accordingly, tailoring his offer to what he thinks the PC wants.
effect.legendary: Varth is fully convinced AND the PC picks up on HOW Varth adjusts — revealing what Varth values in people. A window into his manipulation technique.

scenes.subtitle: Meeting the Admiral

scenes.title: The Deal Gone Bad
scenes.subtitle: Climax
scenes.readAloud:
The landing pad erupts. The Stormtroopers open fire on Skarn's mercenaries. The fortress becomes a three-way warzone — Imperials, the Warlord's forces, and you caught in the middle.

Then a shadow falls across the compound. A shuttle descends through the canopy — sleek, black, unmarked. The ramp drops and a dark figure emerges. Even from a distance, you feel it — a cold pressure in your chest, a wrongness that makes your teeth ache.

Inquisitor Draco has arrived.

The comlink crackles. Maya's voice, tense but steady: "I see the fireworks. Banshee's patched and in the air. Tell me those turrets are down."

readAloudVariants.generatorSabotaged: From the east wing, you hear shouting — Mandrake's voice, barking orders in a language you don't recognize. He's moving. His people are moving. The laser fences are down, the security doors are open, and the turret emplacements sit dark and dead on the walls. Whatever you did to that generator, it worked.
readAloudVariants.generatorIntact: The turret emplacements on the walls are still tracking, barrels sweeping the sky. Maya's going to fly into a kill box. From somewhere in the fortress, you hear Mandrake's voice — frustrated, urgent — still trying to reach his people through locked doors and active fences. The generator is still running. The plan didn't come together.

scenes.gmNotes:
Three-way firefight: Imperials vs. Warlord vs. Heroes. Draco's shuttle deploys Shadow Troopers to cut off retreat. Maya's extraction is CONDITIONAL on turret status.

CONDITIONAL EXTRACTION: If the generator was sabotaged (turrets down), Maya swoops in clean — strafing run across the Imperial perimeter, then landing at the north clearing for pickup. If the turrets are STILL ACTIVE (heroes skipped the sabotage), Maya takes heavy fire on approach. She still comes — she's not leaving them — but the Banshee takes serious damage and the extraction is far more dangerous. The GM should make the turret-down path feel like a reward for honoring Mandrake's deal.

DRACO: Does NOT engage directly. He is glimpsed from distance — a dark figure, a presence. Force-sensitive characters feel nausea and dread. He is here for Varth but arrives too late. This is a preview, not a confrontation. If the heroes try to fight him, he is unbeatable at this level.

MANDRAKE COVERS THE RETREAT: During the extraction, Mandrake and his freed people provide covering fire from the east wing. Mandrake personally covers the heroes' run to the Banshee — laying down suppressive fire, detonating pre-set charges, drawing Shadow Troopers away from the extraction zone.

MANDRAKE'S FATE (DESTINY-GATED): The GM checks the crew's destiny alignment at this point.
• HOPE-DOMINANT: Mandrake breaks contact successfully. As the Banshee lifts off, the heroes see him disappear into the jungle treeline with his people — a silhouette swallowed by green. He lives. He's free. He could return.
• TOLL-DOMINANT: Mandrake is covering the last stretch when Draco's Shadow Troopers pin him down. As the Banshee lifts off, the heroes see Draco — a distant dark figure — ignite a red blade. Mandrake doesn't run. The jungle swallows the light. He dies covering their escape. The cost is real.

EXTRACTION SEQUENCE: The heroes must cross open ground under fire to reach the Banshee. Shadow Troopers flank from the jungle. Skarn's mercenaries are fighting the Imperials but some target the heroes opportunistically. Mandrake's covering fire helps clear the path. Smoke grenades, covering fire, and speed are key.

Once aboard, Maya punches the engines and breaks atmosphere. Jump to hyperspace before the Star Destroyer can scramble TIEs.

VARTH'S PITCH: In the debrief, Varth lays out the next step: 'The money is locked inside Varga's network. I have the key, but the Hutt moved the account after I was captured. We need to get inside his operation.' This sets up Adventure 2.

npcs.name: Inquisitor Valin Draco
npcs.notes: Arrives via shuttle. The campaign's recurring villain. Does not appear in person in this scene — glimpsed from distance.
npcs.behavior: Draco does not engage the heroes directly in this scene. He arrives, deploys his Shadow Troopers, and begins methodically securing the fortress. The heroes see him from a distance — a dark figure descending from the shuttle ramp. This is a preview, not a confrontation.
npcs.intel: Draco is an Inquisitor — a Force-sensitive Imperial operative. He is here specifically for Varth. The heroes will face him directly in later adventures.

maneuver.name: Force Dominion
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Instead of moving, the Controller designates its zone as [Difficult Terrain] for enemies until end of next round. Enemies inside gain [Slowed].

gambit.name: Force Choke
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Restrained] Tactical. Cannot use Move next turn.

exploit.name: Psychic Backlash
exploit.trigger: A PC in the Controller's zone successfully uses a social or Force action.
exploit.description: That PC gains [Disoriented]. The Controller's Force presence disrupts concentration.

threatBuild.notes: NEMESIS. Draco does NOT fight the party in Adv1 — he arrives, demonstrates power, and leaves. This stat block is for the campaign. Defense 7 = max(3,3)-1+4+1. Evasion 5 = 3-1+4-1(mystic). Resist 8 = 5-1+4. 2 actions/round. Boss: N-1 Exploits. He is designed to be unbeatable at this point in the campaign.

npcs.name: Shadow Troopers
npcs.notes: Black-armored elite commandos. Stealth suits. Cutting off retreat.
npcs.behavior: Deployed to the jungle perimeter. They move through the trees like ghosts. Their job is to prevent anyone leaving the fortress alive. They are significantly more dangerous than standard Stormtroopers.
npcs.intel: Shadow Troopers are Inquisitorius assets — black ops. Stealth-equipped, enhanced training, independent targeting.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move up to 2 zones. If the Harrier ends farther from all enemies than it started, +1 Power on next attack.

gambit.name: Pinning Fire
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Slowed] Tactical.

exploit.name: Snap Shot
exploit.trigger: A PC uses Move to enter or cross a zone within weapon range.
exploit.description: Immediately attack that PC at -2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Elite Imperial commandos. Defense 5 = max(2,4)-1+2+2(medium armor). Evasion 4 = 4-1+2-1(medium armor). Resist 4 = Grit 3-1+2. These are the real threat in the finale — the party should feel outmatched.

npcs.name: Maya
npcs.notes: Provides conditional extraction in the Banshee. Still wounded but flying one-handed. Her defining moment in Adventure 1.
npcs.behavior: Strafing runs across Imperial positions. Extraction approach depends on turret status: clean approach if turrets are down, heavy fire if turrets are still up. Calm under pressure despite her wound — this is what she does. After extraction, she's the one flying them to safety.
dialogue: "I see the fireworks. Banshee's patched and in the air. Tell me those turrets are down."
"Turrets are dead? Then I'm coming in hot. Strafing run in three... two... GO! Move to the clearing!"
"Everybody aboard? Hang on — this is going to be fast. And someone check my shoulder — I think I reopened it."
"Star Destroyer is launching fighters. Jumping in thirty seconds whether you're strapped in or not."
npcs.intel: Maya had the Banshee repaired and flight-ready despite her wound. She monitored the Imperial comms and moved when the shooting started. If the turrets are down, her extraction is clean. If the turrets are up, she takes heavy fire but still comes — she's not leaving them.

maneuver.name: Reposition
maneuver.description: Modifies Move. Move up to 2 zones. If the Harrier ends farther from all enemies than it started, +1 Power on next attack.

gambit.name: Pinning Fire
gambit.description: In addition to normal effects, target gains [Slowed] Tactical.

exploit.name: Snap Shot
exploit.trigger: A PC uses Move to enter or cross a zone within weapon range.
exploit.description: Immediately attack that PC at -2 Power.

threatBuild.notes: Maya is not a combat threat. She is a social operator who chose this table on purpose. Her Face kit represents con artistry, not diplomacy.

classification.name: Standard
classification.description: Base template. Full stats, fixed action cadence. The workhorse NPC.
classification.notes: 2-4 Standards represent a meaningful encounter for a party.

threatBuild.name: Maya

scenes.hazards: Three-way firefight. Imperial walkers approaching. Shadow Troopers flanking. Time-critical extraction.

decisions.choice: Fight through to the north clearing (turrets down)
decisions.consequence: Maya extracts clean. Mandrake covers the retreat. The plan worked.

decisions.choice: Fight through to the clearing (turrets still up)
decisions.consequence: Maya takes heavy fire on approach. Banshee is damaged. Extraction is chaotic and costly.

decisions.choice: Flee into the jungle for an alternate pickup
decisions.consequence: Must evade Shadow Troopers in dense canopy. Maya provides strafing support from a distance.

narrativeLinks.note: Varth's reveal about Varga holding the encryption keys launches Adventure 2.

encounters.name: Three-Way Firefight
encounters.trigger: The landing pad erupts as Imperials and Skarn's forces clash
encounters.description: The fortress becomes a warzone. Three factions: Imperials securing the perimeter, Skarn's mercenaries defending their territory, and the heroes trying to escape through the middle.
encounters.tactics: Use the chaos. The Imperials and mercs are fighting each other — the heroes can exploit the confusion to slip through. But Shadow Troopers are specifically hunting for anyone trying to leave.

composition.positioning: Imperials and mercs fighting each other; heroes threading through

encounters.name: Shadow Trooper Ambush
encounters.trigger: When the heroes reach the jungle perimeter
encounters.description: Four Shadow Troopers emerge from concealment in the treeline. Black armor, stealth suits, precision fire. They are the real obstacle to escape.
encounters.tactics: Shadow Troopers fight smart — overlapping fields of fire, tactical movement, targeting the weakest member. They don't charge; they control the engagement distance.

composition.positioning: Concealed in canopy, overlapping fields of fire

encounters.name: Maya's Conditional Extraction
encounters.trigger: After clearing or bypassing the Shadow Troopers
encounters.description: Maya swoops in with the Banshee. If turrets were destroyed (generator sabotaged), she comes in clean — strafing run, then landing at the north clearing. If turrets are still active, she takes heavy fire on approach and the Banshee is badly damaged. Either way, the heroes must reach the clearing and board under fire while Mandrake covers their retreat.
encounters.tactics: Running to the ship while Maya and Mandrake cover them. Someone needs to man the dorsal turret. The ship takes hits during liftoff. Mandrake's fate plays out as the Banshee lifts off — the GM narrates the destiny-gated outcome.

environmentMechanics.name: Three-Way Crossfire
environmentMechanics.effect: Stray fire from all directions. Moving through contested areas without cover risks catching bolts from any faction.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Use Tactics to read the battle. Move during lulls. Use smoke grenades for concealment.

environmentMechanics.name: Shadow Trooper Stealth
environmentMechanics.effect: Shadow Troopers are nearly invisible in the treeline. First shot comes without warning.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Togruta echolocation, Rodian infrared, or Cathar hearing can detect them before visual contact. Investigation check to spot thermal shimmer.

environmentMechanics.name: Imperial Walker Approach
environmentMechanics.trigger: After 3-4 combat rounds in the fortress
environmentMechanics.effect: AT-DP walkers breach the treeline. Heavy weapons fire. The fortress is about to be overrun.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Leave NOW. The walkers make escape on foot nearly impossible.

environmentMechanics.name: Maya's Conditional Extraction
environmentMechanics.effect: If turrets are down: Maya provides clean strafing pass and lands at the north clearing. If turrets are up: Maya takes heavy fire on approach — Banshee is damaged, extraction is chaotic.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Sabotage the generator in Scene 4 to ensure turrets are down. This is the payoff for honoring Mandrake's deal.

environmentMechanics.name: Mandrake's Cover Fire
environmentMechanics.effect: Mandrake and his freed people provide covering fire from the east wing. Mandrake personally covers the heroes' run to the Banshee.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: Mandrake's covering fire suppresses Shadow Troopers for 2 rounds, creating a window to reach the ship.

environmentMechanics.name: Mandrake's Fate (Destiny-Gated)
environmentMechanics.effect: Hope-dominant crew: Mandrake escapes into the jungle with his people. Toll-dominant crew: Draco cuts Mandrake down as the Banshee lifts off. The heroes witness the outcome.
environmentMechanics.mitigation: This is not a choice — it's a consequence of the crew's cumulative destiny alignment.

intel: Inquisitor Draco is real — and terrifying
The Empire invested military resources to hold Varth
Varth's pitch: the money is in Varga's network, need to infiltrate his operation
Maya proved herself under fire — she's the crew's pilot
Mandrake's fate determined by crew destiny alignment
connections: Maya (pilot, partner — proved her worth under fire and wounded)
Vazus Mandrake (fate determined by destiny alignment — potential Act 3 return if Hope-dominant)

pacing.openingBeat: The fortress erupts. Three-way firefight. Mandrake's people moving in the east wing.
pacing.risingAction: Shadow Troopers deploy. Draco arrives. Mandrake covers the retreat. The exit is closing.
pacing.climax: Maya's conditional extraction — turrets down means clean pickup, turrets up means heavy fire. Sprint to the Banshee while Mandrake holds the line.
pacing.resolution: Break atmosphere. Mandrake's fate plays out below — Hope or Toll. Jump to hyperspace. Varth's pitch for the next job.

disciplineChallenges.target: Three-way battle (Imperials vs Mercenaries vs PCs)
disciplineChallenges.context: Reading the three-way battle to find the safest extraction path. Chaos everywhere.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll at the start of the climax. Sets up the escape route.

control.failure: No clear path. They have to fight through at least one engagement.
control.mastery: The PC also identifies which faction is winning — critical for knowing who'll be chasing them.

effect.fleeting: One gap spotted — narrow and closing. They have 2 rounds to reach it.
effect.masterful: A clear gap in the fighting — the heroes can move through with minimal contact.
effect.legendary: Perfect read. The PC identifies a route AND a timing window where both factions are focused on each other. Free passage if they move NOW.

disciplineChallenges.context: Dodging through crossfire between Imperial and mercenary positions. Running and weaving.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Roll when crossing the killzone.

control.failure: Caught in crossfire. Ranged damage from stray shots.

effect.fleeting: Through but singed — minor damage from near misses.
effect.masterful: Clean movement through the kill zone. No hits taken.
effect.legendary: The PC's evasive movement is so effective that they draw fire away from the rest of the party — everyone gets through.

disciplineChallenges.context: Engaging the Shadow Troopers in the treeline. Elite enemies with stealth capabilities.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: In-combat. The hardest fight of the adventure.

control.failure: Shadow Troopers return devastating fire from cover. Heroes are [Suppressed].

effect.fleeting: Suppressive fire forces one Shadow Trooper to relocate. Buys a round of movement.
effect.masterful: Precise fire drops or suppresses them. Path to the clearing opens.
effect.legendary: Headshot. One Shadow Trooper eliminated, the other breaks position. The treeline is clear.

disciplineChallenges.target: Open ground sprint to the Banshee
disciplineChallenges.context: Sprint to Maya's ship across open ground. Final push with wounded, exhausted characters.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: The final check. Make it dramatic.

control.failure: Stumble, exhaustion, or carrying wounded slows the run. More rounds in the open.

effect.fleeting: Staggering run. They make it but it costs — arrive exhausted, last ones aboard.
effect.masterful: Full speed. Board the ship. Go.
effect.legendary: Adrenaline surge. The PC carries a wounded ally AND makes the ramp. Heroic moment.

disciplineChallenges.context: Liftoff under fire. Breaking atmosphere with a Star Destroyer in orbit and TIE fighters scrambling.
disciplineChallenges.narrativePacing: Final roll of the adventure. Go big.

control.failure: Ship takes heavy damage during liftoff. Emergency repairs needed in hyperspace. They escape but battered.
control.mastery: The escape is so clean that the Imperials lose tracking. No pursuit into hyperspace.

effect.fleeting: Rough liftoff. TIE fighters score hits but the Banshee makes it to hyperspace. Hull integrity is concerning.
effect.masterful: Clean escape. Jump to hyperspace before fighters can intercept. The stars streak.
effect.legendary: Perfect escape — the Ace threads through the TIE formation, using the Star Destroyer's own mass shadow to mask the hyperspace vector. Imperials have no idea where they went.
